name: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines

outbound_official.notes: Source is the official Saint Vincent and the Grenadines Ministry of Foreign Affairs, International Trade and Regional Integration outbound visa page (article id=296). The page states: (1) Vincentians are generally exempted from short-stay visa requirements for countries in the Americas, except USA and Canada; (2) Visa-free access to all CARICOM member states; (3) Indefinite stay in OECS member states (Antigua and Barbuda, Dominica, Grenada, Montserrat, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia — all also captured above as CARICOM); (4) United Kingdom — up to 6 months visa-free; (5) Schengen area (28 countries listed) — up to 90 days within a 6-month period under the EU-SVG Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement signed 28 May 2015. The CARICOM list above reflects the 14 full member states (excluding SVG itself). The Americas region exemption is stated broadly; individual non-CARICOM Americas countries are not enumerated by the official source. The page does not mention visa-on-arrival, e-visa, or ETA destinations. No date is published on the official page.

visa_free.notes: The majority of foreign nationals visiting Saint Vincent and the Grenadines are granted visa-free access. Per the Ministry of National Security visitors permit schedule, 'other international countries' receive three (3) months (90 days). The 12 nationalities listed under visa_required require advance visas. US nationals receive 6 months per the Ministry of National Security visitors permit schedule. CORRECTED 2026-06-14: prior record stated 30 days; official visitors permit page (security.gov.vc/id=56) specifies 3 months for 'other international countries'.

Indian ordinary passport holders are not listed among the 12 nationalities required to obtain a pre-entry visa (per MFA Visa Criteria page and Ministry of National Security entry visa page). As 'other international countries', Indian nationals receive a visitor permit of three (3) months (90 days) on arrival, granted by an Immigration Officer. No visa, ETA, or e-visa is required. A diplomatic/official passport exemption agreement also exists (signed 10.09.2019, in force 13.01.2020) for 90 days, but ordinary passport holders are separately covered by the general visa-free policy. Verified 2026-06-14 from official SVG government sources: foreign.gov.vc, security.gov.vc, and gov.vc.

CARICOM nationals are allowed six (6) months visa-free stay. As of 1 October 2025, nationals of Barbados, Belize, and Dominica additionally enjoy full freedom of movement (right to reside, work, and remain indefinitely without a work permit) under the Enhanced Cooperation Protocol to the Revised Treaty of Chaguaramas, alongside SVG itself.

visa_free.nationality: British passport holders and British Overseas Citizens
visa_free.notes: Holders of British passports or British Overseas Citizen status are allowed six (6) months visa-free.

OECS nationals are allowed indefinite stay within Saint Vincent and the Grenadines (must demonstrate sufficient funds). Full OECS freedom of movement applies under the Revised Treaty of Basseterre (OECS Economic Union, in force 2011).

visa_free.nationality: EU and Schengen Area nationals (ordinary passports)
visa_free.notes: Citizens of EU member states and Schengen-associated countries (Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland) holding ordinary passports are admitted visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period for non-employment purposes, under the EU-SVG Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (signed 28 May 2015, in force 1 May 2023). The agreement covers all passport types (ordinary, diplomatic, service/official, and special). EU countries included: Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland. Note: the Ministry of National Security visitors permit schedule groups Schengen countries with 6-month permits, but the governing EU agreement stipulates 90 days in any 180-day period.

United States nationals receive six (6) months visa-free stay per the Ministry of National Security visitors permit schedule. This is consistent with treatment of UK and CARICOM nationals in the same schedule.

visa_policy.notes: The following 12 nationalities REQUIRE a visa to enter Saint Vincent and the Grenadines per the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Visa Criteria page: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Cameroon, China, Dominican Republic, Haiti, Iran, Iraq, Lebanon, Nepal, Nigeria, and Syria. Note: the Ministry of National Security entry visa page and gov.vc visa requirements page list only 10 (omitting Bangladesh and Nepal); the MFA visa criteria page is the most specific and lists all 12. Visa cost: EC$200.00 (non-refundable). No visa-on-arrival, e-visa, or ETA program was found on official sources. The visitors permit page at security.gov.vc shows USA and Schengen nationals receive 6-month permits; EU/Schengen nationals are however subject to the 90-day/180-day rule under the bilateral EU-SVG treaty.

visa_required.notes: Per MFA Visa Criteria page: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Cameroon, China, Dominican Republic, Haiti, Iran, Iraq, Lebanon, Nepal, Nigeria, Syria. The Ministry of National Security entry visa page lists only 10 (omitting Bangladesh and Nepal). Both pages are official; the discrepancy between SVG's own official sources is noted in gaps.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Indian diplomatic or official passports may enter Saint Vincent and the Grenadines visa-free for up to 90 days. Agreement signed 10.09.2019, entered into force 13.01.2020. Does not extend to ordinary or service passport holders (who are separately covered by the general visa-free policy as 'other international countries').

cbi.notes: As of June 2026, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines does NOT have an active Citizenship by Investment program. The official Prime Minister's Office citizenship page states: 'More information regarding Citizenship by Investment requirements will be made available shortly.' Following the NDP election victory in November 2025, Prime Minister Goodwin Friday's February 2026 Budget Address confirmed a CBI program with a mandatory residency component and ring-fenced investment fund, targeting a mid-2026 launch. However, no enabling legislation, official investment routes, minimum amounts, or processing timelines have been published on any official government domain as of the validation date. The program is not yet formally established.

agreements: CARICOM freedom of movement (6 months visa-free for all CARICOM nationals)
CARICOM Enhanced Cooperation Protocol — Barbados, Belize, Dominica, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines: full freedom of movement (right to reside and work without work permit), in effect 1 October 2025
OECS freedom of movement (Revised Treaty of Basseterre, OECS Economic Union, in force 2011) — indefinite stay for OECS nationals

EU-SVG Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (signed 28 May 2015, in force 1 May 2023) — covers all passport types (ordinary, diplomatic, service, official, special) of EU and Schengen-area nationals; 90 days in any 180-day period
India-SVG Visa Exemption Agreement for holders of Diplomatic and Official Passports (signed 10 September 2019, in force 13 January 2020)

data_quality.validation_notes: Validated June 2026. Changes from prior record: (1) Added EU/Schengen ordinary passport holders explicitly to visa_free with 90-day max_stay per the EU-SVG treaty; they were previously only noted in agreements but omitted from visa_free entries. (2) Added United States nationals explicitly to visa_free with 180-day max_stay per security.gov.vc visitors permit schedule; this was omitted from the prior record despite being on the official visitors permit page. (3) Added visa_required block (default_visa_free:true with list of 12 nationalities) to formally capture the inversion structure. (4) Updated CBI notes to reflect the February 2026 Budget Address announcement by PM Goodwin Friday (NDP) of a mid-2026 CBI launch with mandatory residency; official page still says 'coming shortly' with no formal program published. (5) Updated CARICOM enhanced freedom-of-movement note to clarify it is a four-territory arrangement (Barbados, Belize, Dominica, SVG) not a full-CARICOM rule. (6) Corrected the prior record statement that the visa-required list contained 'Bangladesh and Nepal' on some pages but not others — the MFA visa criteria page explicitly lists all 12 including BGD and NPL; the discrepancy with the MoNSec page is preserved in gaps. 2026-06-14: Added explicit India (IND) entry to visa_free with 90-day max_stay (ordinary passport holders covered by general visa-free default as 'other international countries' per security.gov.vc/id=56 visitors permit schedule); corrected general default max_stay_days from 30 to 90 days per the same official page; India was previously only captured under conditional_access for diplomatic/official passports — ordinary passport India entry is now explicit. Source: security.gov.vc (official). Airline aggregator claim of visa-on-arrival is incorrect; correct classification is visa_free with no prior registration or fee required.
gaps: The official visa policy pages do not enumerate all visa-free nationalities individually; only the 12 visa-required nationalities are listed by the MFA, with the remainder described as 'most foreign nationals' receiving 1 month visa-free.

Bangladesh and Nepal appear on the Ministry of Foreign Affairs visa criteria page but NOT on the Ministry of National Security entry visa application page or the gov.vc visa requirements page. Both pages are official; the discrepancy between SVG's own official sources is unresolved.

The Ministry of National Security visitors permit page groups Schengen nationals with a 6-month permit, but the governing EU-SVG treaty specifies 90 days in any 180-day period. Which figure immigration officers actually enforce at the port of entry is unclear.
No official breakdown of visa-free access by specific country or region (beyond CARICOM, OECS, British, EU/Schengen, and US nationals) was found on official sources.
No e-visa, visa-on-arrival, or ETA system was found; absence confirmed by official immigration pages.
No official Residency by Investment (RBI) or golden visa program found on official sources.
No official digital nomad visa or skilled worker fast-track scheme found on official sources.
CBI program announced in the February 2026 Budget Address with a mid-2026 target but not yet formally established with published legislation or official investment routes as of June 2026.

No official SVG government source found specifying conditional entry based on held third-country credentials (e.g., US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, Canadian visa, residence permits, Green Card). Confirmed absent after research covering foreign.gov.vc, security.gov.vc, svgconsulate.vc, svghighcom.co.uk, gov.uk/FCDO, and US State Dept.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder-specific exemption rule found on any official SVG or Indian government source.

Other potential bilateral visa waiver agreements for diplomatic/service/official passports (beyond India) could not be confirmed from official SVG government sources; India MEA confirms the India-SVG diplomatic/official agreement as the only such agreement found on official sources.

sources_checked.description: Ministry of Foreign Affairs, International Trade and Regional Integration - Visas page; lists general 1-month rule, CARICOM 6-month, British 6-month, OECS indefinite; lists 10 visa-required nationalities (no Bangladesh/Nepal); EU agreement mentioned with 90-day rule and list of Schengen countries

Ministry of Foreign Affairs - Visa Criteria page; explicitly lists 12 visa-required nationalities including Bangladesh and Nepal; explains security and document-verification basis for visa requirements
Government of Saint Vincent and the Grenadines - Visa Requirements; lists only 10 visa-required nationalities (no Bangladesh/Nepal); no other entry rules listed

Ministry of National Security - Visitors Permit page; lists permit durations by nationality group: CARICOM 6 months, UK 6 months, Schengen 6 months, USA 6 months, other international 3 months; OECS nationals indefinite. This is the authoritative source for India ordinary passport holders as 'other international countries' receiving 90 days.
Ministry of National Security - Entry Visa Requirements; lists 10 visa-required nationalities (no Bangladesh/Nepal); India not listed; no credential-based conditional rules found
Ministry of National Security - Entry Visa Application Form
Office of the Prime Minister - Citizenship page; CBI listed as 'coming soon' with no investment amounts or routes published; standard citizenship fees: $150 application + $1,500/$2,200 registration

Government of SVG - Complete Freedom of Movement in Four CARICOM Territories (October 2025); confirms Barbados, Belize, Dominica and SVG four-territory enhanced freedom-of-movement effective 1 October 2025

India Ministry of External Affairs - Countries with Visa Exemption Agreement for Diplomatic/Official/Service Passport holders; SVG entry: diplomatic and official passports, 90 days, signed 10.09.2019, in force 13.01.2020

EUR-Lex - Visa waiver agreements summary; confirms EU-SVG agreement covers all passport types (ordinary, diplomatic, service/official, special); 90 days in any 180-day period; signed 2015, in force 1 May 2023 (OJ L 105, 20.4.2023)

Ministry of Foreign Affairs - Outbound visa information for Vincentian citizens; states: Americas generally exempt except USA and Canada; CARICOM visa-free; OECS indefinite stay; UK up to 6 months; Schengen 90 days in 6 months. This is the origin-side official outbound source-of-record.
